fa: First President, set two-term precedent, Commander-in-Chief during Revolutionary War
First vice president to become president, avoided war with France
Author of Declaration of Independence, Louisiana Purchase doubled US territory
Father of the Constitution, led US through War of 1812
Era of Good Feelings, Monroe Doctrine warned Europe against colonialism
Son of 2nd president, promoted infrastructure and education

vp: John C. Calhoun / Martin Van Buren
fa: First Democrat, Indian Removal Act, faced nullification crisis
Faced Panic of 1837, first president born as a US citizen
Shortest presidency (31 days), died of pneumonia
First VP to succeed a president, annexed Texas
Expanded US to Pacific, acquired California and Southwest from Mexico
War hero of Mexican-American War, died 16 months into term
Signed Compromise of 1850, sent Perry to open Japan
Kansas-Nebraska Act inflamed slavery debate
Only bachelor president, failed to prevent Civil War
Led US through Civil War, abolished slavery, assassinated by John Wilkes Booth
First impeached president, Reconstruction controversies
Union general who won Civil War, Reconstruction era president
Ended Reconstruction, disputed election decided by Congress
Assassinated after only 200 days in office
Pendleton Civil Service Reform Act, modernized US Navy
Only president to serve two non-consecutive terms (22nd and 24th)
Six states admitted to Union, first billion-dollar Congress
Only president to serve two non-consecutive terms, faced Panic of 1893
Spanish-American War made US a world power, assassinated in 1901
Trust-busting, conservation of national parks, Nobel Peace Prize, Panama Canal
Only person to serve as both President and Chief Justice of Supreme Court
Led US in WWI, proposed League of Nations, 14 Points for peace
Teapot Dome Scandal, died in office
Roaring Twenties prosperity, known for minimal government intervention
Great Depression began under his watch, opposed direct federal relief

vp: John Nance Garner / Henry A. Wallace / Harry S. Truman
fa: New Deal, led US through WWII, only president elected 4 times
Ended WWII with atomic bombs, Marshall Plan, Korean War, NATO
WWII Supreme Commander, Interstate Highway System, warned of military-industrial complex
Cuban Missile Crisis, Space Race, assassinated in Dallas (1963)
Great Society, Civil Rights Act, Voting Rights Act, Vietnam War escalation
Opened China relations, ended Vietnam War, resigned due to Watergate scandal
Only president never elected VP or President, pardoned Nixon
Camp David Accords, Iran hostage crisis, Nobel Peace Prize (2002)
Reaganomics, ended Cold War, Iran-Contra affair
Gulf War, German reunification, fall of Soviet Union
Economic boom, balanced budget, impeached but not removed from office
9/11 response, War on Terror, Iraq War, Hurricane Katrina
First African-American president, Affordable Care Act, killed Bin Laden
America First policy, impeached twice, COVID-19 pandemic
Oldest elected president, withdrew from Afghanistan, Ukraine support
Only president to serve two non-consecutive terms (45th and 47th)
